reviews.review_msg: Gohram was a good lecturer in hindsight; however, he was unorganized, said conflicting things when it came to grading after he made a major mistake, and flat-out refused to take accountability when asked about it. I enjoyed the class and learned a lot, but he came off as difficult at the end.

Gohram is genuinely just a great dude and a great prof. Clearly cares a lot about student success and put a lot of work into the course. Grades are mostly determined by the midterm and group presentation so work hard on those. Overall class was pretty easy.
Best prof ever I haven taken at SFU Beedie! Never miss this A+ section everyone!

He gave me the first A+ in my BBA life (last term, with a 373)! I heard my friends say he is coming back and going to teach again, just come to write it to my friends! GO GO GO! If you did not choose his sections, you will regret it forever, so PLEASE CHOOSE HIM, Buddy!

I could not imagine how he could be so effective. He changed my mind to choose the concentration in Operations Management, provided examples in a straightforward and easy-to-understand way, and the exams are just that. (P.S.: You do not need to practice a lot to get good grades.) I LOVE his courses.

Extraordinarily pleasant, he's a model for all Beedie faculty ~ he doesn't teach at SFU often, so if you see his name, don't hesitate to grab it! Quick! You have to be quick, in our classes he gave so many A's and B's above!

I have to compliment this professor! Took BUS336 & BUS373 with him, both were the “Easiest 100%” exams, and you didn't even have to travel to the classroom to learn well. Average didn't affect the ratio of A's - B+'s he gave! Best professor in life, bar none！！！！

He completed his course about a year ago for BUS 373, and the exam was very easy, similar to childhood calculations and super straightforward. It's easy to score full marks, and he gave more A's than other faculty, so please choose him without any doubts.
The assignments are very easy, provide free credits, and help you apply what you've learned in real-world situations. Office hours are extremely helpful — he is always patient and approachable.
I enjoy his teaching style, which offers very helpful instructions. Please work with him! Other 373 instructors are not good to study with, he is the GOAT!
Best instructor I have ever met at SFU, he is the best choice for this course!

Thank you for being such a great teacher! You seem to have a gift for explaining difficult topics very well, and your passion for the subject made me enjoy it more. I really appreciate all of your help inside and outside the classroom, and I'm glad I got to take another class with you.

Best prof for bus 336!! He actually wants you to learn, lectures were easy to understand, and he provides practice exams. Real world applications in each lecture. I actually feel like I might use what I learned in this class. He uses R and Tableau. 40% of your grade are take-home assignments, and 2 midterms 25%, plus a group hackathon.
Definitely will take any other classes he teaches. Very understanding and wants students to succeed, exams were from practice papers.

Lectures are recorded and posted online after class. 2 midterms (30% each), 6 hw assignments (30% total), and ~10 in-class assignments (1% each). He does use R in this class for coding but you could always refer to his slides/lectures for help (or chatgpt).  Pretty good for a newer prof. Would take again. Class average is ~75.
